message:
fix(fcp-cbor): SchemaId separator-collision (REVIEW-A9, mzi9x)

SchemaId::hash concatenated `namespace || ':' || name || '@' || version` raw,
so distinct tuples could alias to the same SchemaHash — namespace=`a:b`,
name=`c` and namespace=`a`, name=`b:c` both hashed `a:b:c@1.0.0`. Same flaw in
as_bytes (the canonical string form). This breaks schema-binding uniqueness for
canonical payload prefixes and opens a cross-schema replay/aliasing window.

Fix is two layers:

1. hash() now feeds each component (namespace, name, version-string) into
   BLAKE3 with a u64-LE length prefix. Distinct (namespace, name, version)
   tuples can no longer alias even when a SchemaId is constructed via the
   public fields, bypassing the constructor.

2. SchemaId::new / SchemaId::try_new reject ':' and '@' in namespace/name via
   a new SchemaIdError::ReservedSeparator. The canonical as_bytes form
   ("{ns}:{name}@{version}") is now unambiguous by construction.

Three pre-existing tests that locked in the vulnerable behavior
(schema_id_as_bytes_colon_in_namespace, _at_sign_in_name,
_with_only_separator_chars) are converted into rejection tests against
try_new. Added schema_id_hash_does_not_collide_across_separator_aliases and
schema_id_hash_uses_length_prefixed_encoding to lock in the new invariant by
constructing aliased pairs via direct struct literal (the path the hash must
defend against).

The CanonicalPayload + ObjectId golden vectors in fcp-conformance are updated
to the new length-prefixed schema hash; ObjectId derivation feeds schema.hash
so it shifts in lockstep.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-cbor → 471 lib + 8 golden tests pass;
cargo test -p fcp-conformance --lib vectors::core → 49/49 pass;
cargo clippy -p fcp-cbor --lib -- -D warnings clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
